title: acer oliverianum var. tutcheri
intro: Acer oliverianum var. tutcheri, also known as Oliver's maple, is a rare and lesser-known variety of maple tree native to China. Traditionally, the plant's bark and leaves are used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oliver's maple are believed to include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
